edges.description: Progenitor injury suppresses marrow output, particularly the neutrophil lineage.
edges.source: Cytotoxic chemotherapy exposure and hematopoietic progenitor injury

edges.description: Suppressed granulopoiesis lowers the circulating neutrophil count.
A low neutrophil count raises the risk of infection and febrile neutropenia.

nodes.description: Loss of functional progenitors suppresses bone marrow hematopoietic output. In this entry the suppression is viewed through its effect on granulopoiesis, reducing the marrow's capacity to replace circulating neutrophils.

evidence.snippet: The resultant bone marrow suppression is a major dose-limiting side effect.

nodes.description: Myelosuppressive chemotherapy delivers cytotoxic drugs to the bone marrow, where rapidly proliferating hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells are preferentially injured — the conserved initiating lesion of the myelosuppression module, here viewed through its granulocytic consequence.
nodes.id: Cytotoxic chemotherapy exposure and hematopoietic progenitor injury

evidence.snippet: Proliferating hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells (HSPCs) in the bone marrow are particularly susceptible to chemotherapy-induced damage.
Chemotherapy-induced damage of hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells (HSPC) causes multi-lineage myelosuppression.

nodes.description: Neutropenia impairs host defense, raising the risk of infection and febrile neutropenia — the principal driver of morbidity and mortality and the reason chemotherapy doses are delayed or reduced.

evidence.snippet: Chemotherapy-induced neutropenia is a major risk factor for infection-related morbidity and mortality and also a significant dose-limiting toxicity in cancer treatment.
Neutropenia increases infection risk, and anemia frequently results in debilitating fatigue.

nodes.description: Reduced marrow output lowers the circulating neutrophil count. This node specializes the module's multilineage-cytopenia effector to the neutrophil lineage, the predominant and most clinically consequential cytopenia for many regimens.

evidence.snippet: one of the most common side effects of chemotherapy is myelosuppression, which typically manifests as neutropenia, anemia, thrombocytopenia, and/or lymphopenia

meta.association: Reduced-function UGT1A1 variants increase irinotecan-associated neutropenia risk

evidence.snippet: screening for UGT1A1*28 polymorphism may identify patients with lower SN-38 glucuronidation rates and greater susceptibility to irinotecan induced gastrointestinal and bone marrow toxicity.

nodes.description: Promoter repeat allele associated with lower SN-38 glucuronidation and increased irinotecan myelotoxicity susceptibility.
